name: Interface Synthesizer
description: Interface for synthesizing OpenBindings interfaces from binding artifacts. Given a source artifact governed by a binding specification (e.g., an OpenAPI document), an interface synthesizer extracts operations, schemas, sources, and bindings into an OBI document.

BindingSpecInfo.description: Information about a supported binding specification.

bindingSpec.description: Binding specification identifier, exact and opaque (e.g., openbindings.openapi@1, openbindings.mcp@1).

description.description: Human-readable description of this binding specification.

SynthesizeInterfaceSource.description: A binding source to synthesize an interface from.

bindingSpec.description: Binding specification identifier governing this source.

name.description: Key name for this binding source in the output.

location.description: Location to fetch content from (path or URL).

outputLocation.description: Location to reference in the output.

embed.description: If true, embed content inline in the output.

SynthesisCoverageEntry.description: Disposition of one source interaction unit or separately tracked projection observed during synthesis. An interaction unit is an addressable target or independently selectable alternative whose omission would remove an upstream-permitted invocation path; a projection records schema or semantic fidelity of a represented path.

sourceIndex.description: Zero-based index of the input source containing the interaction unit.

sourceKey.description: Key of the corresponding source in the emitted interface. Required when status is represented or lossy.

sourceRef.description: Stable source-local identifier for the interaction unit. It need not be a conformant binding ref when the unit is excluded or invalid.

scope.description: Whether the unit is an addressable target, an independently selectable alternative of a target, or a schema/semantic projection whose fidelity is assessed separately.

operationKey.description: Emitted operation key. Required when status is represented or lossy.

bindingKey.description: Emitted binding key. Required when status is represented or lossy.

bindingRef.description: Emitted binding ref. Required when status is represented or lossy; the empty string denotes a binding-specification-defined omitted ref.

reasonCode.description: Stable family-namespaced reason code. Required unless status is represented.

rule.description: Binding-specification rule or section governing the disposition, when applicable.

requirements.description: Named runtime prerequisites for a represented unit, such as credentials or a required codec.

SynthesisCoverage.description: Durable accounting of the source interaction units observed by one synthesis call.

exhaustive.description: True only when every interaction unit in every accepted input source, under the governing binding revision's documented inventory, has exactly one disposition. False when the implementation cannot establish that claim.

fullyRepresented.description: True when exhaustive is true and every upstream-valid interaction unit is represented without a lossy or unsupported disposition. MUST agree with entries.

CoverageLimitation.description: Machine-readable evidence explaining why the synthesis inventory is not exhaustive.

message.description: Human-readable explanation of what may be missing and why.

details.description: Optional implementation- or family-specific structured evidence.

SynthesizeInterfaceResult.description: A creation-time-sound OBI and durable coverage evidence derived from the same synthesis observation.

openbindings.interface-synthesizer.listB.description: List the binding specifications this synthesizer can synthesize interfaces from, by exact identifier.

openbindings.interface-synthesizer.synth.description: Synthesize a creation-time-sound OpenBindings interface, optionally from binding source artifacts. Every emitted binding resolves to a supported target and admits a faithful invocation path. For every accepted source input, represent every callable target admitted by its governing binding revision; an implementation may refuse an unsupported artifact as a whole but MUST refuse instead of silently returning a partial interface when one admitted target cannot be represented faithfully.

Synthesize a creation-time-sound OpenBindings interface and return durable dispositions plus an explicit exhaustiveness claim for the source interaction units considered by the same call. The result distinguishes binding-specification exclusions, invalid source units, lossy projections, and implementation gaps. Coverage is evidence that consumers may verify independently, not proof.
